selected_important_turn.text: Sorry, I sent my last message inadvertently. I meant to say that that sounds great! Yes, I can understand the need for extra water for the hike. With the size of my family, I could use a bit of extra food for our trip.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn shifts the exchange from the opponent's unilateral need-claim about water into a cooperative frame by acknowledging their need and stating your own. It creates the key integrative possibility (trade food for water) that the rest of the deal hinges on.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The message mixes an accidental-apology, empathy, and a vague request. The apology and filler language dilute the leverage of a timely trade-opening; the request for "a bit of extra food" is underspecified (no quantities, no willingness to concede on other items) and doesn't probe the opponent's minimum needs for water. That leaves ambiguity that slows efficient deal-shaping and misses an opportunity to structure a clear, mutually beneficial proposal.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Be concise and shape a clear, actionable trade or information-seeking question: acknowledge their water need briefly, state a specific food request or a menu of options, and ask about their minimum water requirement or willingness to trade other items (e.g., firewood) as contingency. For example, confirm their water minimum and propose explicit trade ratios or alternatives rather than a vague "bit of extra food."

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — clearer, quantified proposals open room for faster integrative trades and avoid wasted back-and-forth.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — explicit options let both sides see and pick balanced splits rather than leaving implicit assumptions.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — fewer ambiguities make it easier to move to concrete offers and agreement.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — concise empathy plus concrete trade signals competence and respect, building trust.

value2reason.Low: I have plenty of water, but could use a little additional for my family
value2reason.Medium: I am in decent shape with firewood, but could use additional to cook the food
value2reason.High: I have a large family and could really use additional food

value2reason.Low: I brought my axe and will not need extra firewood since I can cut my own.
value2reason.Medium: I have some food, but my daughter might need more.
value2reason.High: I am bringing my daughter along and will need extra water for her.

dialogue.text: Hi how are you doing today?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm doing well. I'm very excited about our camping trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I am too! I have a rather large family and we are looking forward to getting out for some time away!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: How nice. I am bringing my daughter and her girl scout troop. I am the co leader.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: We will need some extra water for all the girls on the hike.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Sorry, I sent my last message inadvertently. I meant to say that that sounds great! Yes, I can understand the need for extra water for the hike. With the size of my family, I could use a bit of extra food for our trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Since I'm bringing my daughter and her troop, I really need the extra water. How about we split the 3 water packages 2-1? I'll take 2, and you can have 1. For food, I can give you 2 packages, and I'll keep 1. As for firewood, I don’t need any extra, so you can have all 3.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand the need for water, but with my family, I really need more food. How about we trade 1 water package for 1 food package? What’s your minimum need for water, and are you open to trading firewood as well?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm happy to trade 1 water for 1 food, but I need at least 2 water for my daughter. Let's try 2 water for 1 food, and I'll give you 1 firewood as well. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 2 water for 1 food and 1 firewood. That gives me 1 food and 1 firewood, which aligns with my needs. Let’s confirm the split: you get 2 water, 1 food, 1 firewood; I get 1 water, 2 food, 2 firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds fair. Let me just confirm: I get 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood, while you get 2 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood. I think we’re all set!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Agreed! Final split: I get 2 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood; you get 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood. Safe and enjoyable trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
